title: A.I video creator
description:
You will be creating AI-generated images and videos using tools like Veo3 and editing them with capcut.



You will be trained on everything through our full in-house training course. Previous experience with AI image and video generation is required. You'll make 3 videos per day, and work Monday to Saturday 5-6 hours/day.



You will be rewarded generously with bonuses when videos go viral. I have over 2,000,000+ followers so good quality videos go viral regularly.



REQUIREMENTS



• Previous experience generating AI images and AI videos

• Previous experience with Veo3

• Can work Monday to Saturday, 5–6 hours/day 

• Strong English

• Strong internet connection

• Good eye for quality

• Able to take direction and constructive feedback

• Able to receive payments through Wise



HOW TO APPLY



Record ONE video answering all the questions below. Upload it along with one AI video you've previously made to a Google Drive folder and submit the link to me in your application. 



If you do not submit a video of yourself answering these questions and submit one previously made A.I video your application will NOT be reviewed.



APPLICATION QUESTIONS:



1. Tell us about yourself.

2. Are you in school or currently working? 

3. If not working — why did you leave or get let go from your last position?

4. Can you commit to Monday–Saturday, 5–6 hours/day?

5. What AI tools have you used and what kind of videos have you made?

6. Walk us through how you created an A.I video before.

title: Direct Response Video Editor — eCom Ads (Facebook / Instagram)
description:
We are a fast-growing direct response skincare brand selling in Germany. We run paid ads on
 and Instagram at high volume and we need an editor who understands how content actually performs, not just how it looks.

What you will be doing:



Editing 5 to 10 videos per day

Formats range from 30 seconds to 5 minutes: UGC, native, talking head, AI-generated footage

You will receive complete briefs: script, B-roll, references, and examples of winning ads

You must understand what makes content feel native on Instagram and TikTok: pacing, captions, cuts, sound design



Tools you will use:



Your primary editing software (Premiere, CapCut, DaVinci or equivalent)

Vmake: access provided by our company

Higgsfield: access provided by our company

HeyGen: access provided by our company

ElevenLabs: access provided by our company

Comfort with AI-generated footage and AI voiceover is required. You will be working with it regularly.



What we need from you:



Proven experience editing direct response ads or high-volume UGC content

Deep understanding of Instagram and TikTok content codes. You know why some videos stop the scroll and others do not

Ability to follow a brief precisely and deliver on time every day

Common sense. If something looks off, you flag it before delivering. You do not wait to be told the obvious.

Someone determined, autonomous, and hungry to grow with a brand that is scaling fast



What this job is not:



This is not a creative direction role. Briefs are provided, you execute

This is not a wedding or corporate video role

If you cannot deliver 5 to 10 videos per day consistently, this is not the right fit

If you need your hand held for every small decision, this is not the right fit



What we offer:



900 USD per month

Performance bonuses for editors whose videos become winning ads

Real growth opportunity. We promote from within and reward people who perform



To apply:

Send us two to three examples of direct response or UGC ads you have edited. Tell us your daily output in your last role. Any application without examples will not be considered.

title: Executive Assistant & Customer Success Specialist (SaaS)
description:
Job Title: Executive Assistant and Customer Success Specialist (SaaS)



- Location: Remote (Work from Home)

- Type: Full-Time (40 hours/week)

- Salary: $7.00 – $8.00 USD/hour (Negotiable based on experience)

- Experience: Minimum 2+ years in a similar role



The Role:

We are looking for a high-energy, tech-savvy Executive Assistant to become the right hand to our company founder and the face of our customer service. This isn't just an admin role; you will own the entire customer experience for our software, bridging the gap between our users and our development team.



You must be fluent in English, professional on camera, and possess the tech-intuition to learn new software inside and out within days, not weeks.



Key Responsibilities:

- Customer Success and Support: Take full ownership of the software’s customer service. Resolve tickets, engage with users, and ensure a world-class experience.

- Client Interface: Jump on Zoom calls with clients to provide support, demos, or troubleshooting with confidence and a likable personality.

- Bridge to Dev Team: Identify, document, and report bugs to our tech team. You will be the translator between user feedback and technical fixes.

- Documentation and SOPs: You are an organization wizard. You will create and maintain Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs) and keep our FAQs/Help Center updated and easy to navigate.

- Executive Support: Manage schedules and administrative tasks for the Lead Hacker to ensure maximum efficiency.



Requirements:

- 2+ Years Experience in an EA or Customer Success role (SaaS experience is a major bonus).

- Perfect English: Exceptional verbal and written communication skills are non-negotiable.

- Tech-Fluent: You understand how software works and can talk to developers without getting lost.

- Hyper-Organized: You find joy in documentation, clean folders, and efficient workflows.

- Professional Presence: You are well-presented and personable on video calls.

- Quick Learner: You can grasp complex software features rapidly.

- Bonus Points If... 

     You have a background in Project Management.

     You have previously worked for a SaaS startup.

     You have experience with tools like Slack, Jira, Notion, or Zendesk.



Why Join Us?

- Immediate start for the right candidate.

- Stable, long-term full-time employment.

- Opportunity to work closely with a Lead Hacker and grow within a tech-driven environment.



How to Apply:

- Please send your resume along with a brief (1-2 minute) Loom video introducing yourself. We want to see your personality and hear your English fluency.

- Note: Applications without a video or a personalized cover letter will not be considered.

title: Full-Time Video Editor and Social Media Manager
description:
Full-Time Video Editor and Social Media Manager

Location Remote or Hybrid

Type Full-Time



Overview

We are looking for a high-output, creative video editor and social media operator to handle the full pipeline of content from Twitch streams to viral clips.



This role is not just about cutting footage. You will be responsible for turning raw VODs into engaging, high-retention content across YouTube, TikTok, Instagram,
 or X, and more.



You must understand internet humor, pacing, and audience retention at a high level. This includes 
 timing, comedic edits, and knowing what actually performs on modern platforms.



What You Will Do

Edit Twitch VODs into long-form YouTube videos

Create short-form vertical content for TikTok, Reels, and Shorts

Identify and extract high-performing, viral-worthy 
nts

Apply fast-paced, retention-focused editing with quick cuts, zooms, subtitles, sound effects, and 
 overlays

Repurpose content into multiple formats optimized for each platform

Schedule and post content across all social platforms

Maintain a consistent posting cadence

Handle thumbnails, captions, hashtags, and upload formatting

Track performance and iterate based on what works



Editing Style Requirements

You must be comfortable editing in a fast-paced, humor-driven style similar to the following:







We are specifically looking for:

Fast-paced editing with minimal dead air

Strong comedic timing and punchline delivery

Heavy use of subtitles, sound effects, and visual emphasis


-aware editing that feels current and not outdated



Culture Fit Important

You cannot be sensitive to humor

Content may include dark humor, offensive jokes, and unfiltered commentary

You must understand the difference between humor and malice

If you are easily offended, this is not the role for you



Requirements

Proven experience editing gaming or Twitch content

Experience creating short-form vertical videos

Strong understanding of TikTok, Shorts, and Reels algorithms

Proficiency in Premiere Pro, After Effects, or similar tools

Ability to work fast without sacrificing quality

Ability to consistently deliver content on schedule



Bonus Skills

Thumbnail design

Motion graphics

Experience managing or growing social media accounts



What Success Looks Like

Consistent output of daily or near-daily content

Videos that retain attention, get shared, and grow the audience

Ability to predict what content will perform before posting



Compensation

Competitive salary based on experience

Optional performance bonuses tied to content growth



How to Apply

Submit your portfolio

Include examples of long-form and short-form edits

Optional sample edit from one of our VODs

title: Operations Manager – CEO Support (Remote – Philippines)
description:
About the Role

We are looking for a highly experienced Operations Manager to provide dedicated support to the CEO of a growing U.S.-based fintech company. This is a senior management role responsible for leading operations, managing executive communications, coordinating internal and external stakeholders, and ensuring smooth day-to-day operations. You will work directly with leadership in a high-volume, fast-paced environment where proactiveness and ownership are non-negotiable.



What You Will Do

• Manage the CEO’s calendar — scheduling with internal teams, lenders, and merchants

• Own the inbox: monitor, triage, and respond to 50+
s daily with accuracy and professionalism

• Serve as the communication hub between leadership and all internal/external partners

• Track open items, action plans, and deadlines to ensure consistent follow-through

• Maintain CRM records in HubSpot and documentation in Confluence / Google Docs

• Prepare internal reports, summaries, and status updates for the CEO

• Handle day-to-day operational tasks and reduce the CEO’s administrative burden



What We Require

• Minimum 5 years of experience as an Operations Manager, Chief of Staff, Director of Operations, or in a similar senior operations leadership role

• REQUIRED: Background in financial services, fintech, or lending

• PREFERRED: Experience working with U.S.-based companies

• Excellent written and verbal English (near-native level required)

• Proven ability to manage high-volume
 communication independently

• Strong proficiency in HubSpot, Google Workspace, Slack, Confluence, and AI tools

• High sense of accountability, confidentiality, and independent decision-making

• Available Mon–Fri, 9 AM–5 PM Eastern Time (U.S. hours)



What We Offer

• Fully remote, stable long-term employment with a growing U.S.-based company

• Direct exposure to executive leadership and business operations

• Opportunity to build and own the operations function from the ground up

• Career growth potential as the function is expected to expand over time

• A fast-paced, results-driven culture that values ownership and accountability

title: Executive / Virtual / Personal Assistant
description:
Virtual Assistant / Executive Assistant (Remote, Part-Time, Full-Time Growth)

 

We’re seeking a proactive, highly organized Virtual Assistant to support an entrepreneur with a dynamic lifestyle and multiple business interests. This role spans travel coordination, financial admin, personal projects, digital content support, and more. Ideal for someone resourceful, tech-savvy, and detail-oriented. 



This is a remote, part-time position (20 hours/week with growth to full-time, benefits and bonuses). Requiring 1-2 hours overlap with Pacific Time when it’s needed to reach out to businesses that may only be open 8-5 PST for example. Typically we’d have 1 video meeting per week on Mondays to discuss the priorities and other work is flexible to be completed on your own time with due dates in mind. 



 

Key Responsibilities

• Manage calendars, inboxes, scheduling, prioritization, and follow-through

• Coordinate travel including flights, hotels, transportation, itineraries

• Support property-related needs such as short term airbnb management, scheduling vendors, services, invoices, and monitoring recurring tasks.

• Track and organize expenses; maintain spreadsheet-based reports

• Conduct online research for vendors, pricing, products, market data, and personal projects

• Order essential items, track deliveries/returns, and coordinate gifts and services when needed

• Book personal appointments - health, wellness,, dining, etc.

• Administrative tasks including streamlining personal and business systems

• Learn new tools independently and proactively research solutions without needing step-by-step instruction



 

Requirements

• Excellent spoken and written English with clear, professional communication.

• Prior experience with customer service, spreadsheets, research, travel planning, or financial admin is strongly preferred.

• Highly organized, detail-oriented, consistent, and dependable with reliable internet and computer access.

• Strong Google Workspace proficiency—especially Sheets (formulas, formatting, data cleanup; bonus if pivot tables are familiar).

• Resourceful problem solver—able to complete tasks independently, including entirely new tasks by researching how to do them

• Comfortable using AI tools with the understanding that all outputs must be verified, fact-checked, and properly organized before delivery

• Enjoys learning new tools: Google Workspace, Asana, Claude, ChatGPT, Canva, financial and travel platforms.

• High integrity, ownership mindset, and commitment to delivering accurate, timely, polished work.

• Ability to work autonomously while being accessible for periodic check-ins (e.g., short AM/PM PST  windows).

 

Benefits

• Part-time start (20h first month) with plans to grow into a long-term, full-time role within 1-3 months

• Flexible remote work with weekly check-ins to align the agenda for the week

• Opportunity to learn new systems, take on higher-level projects, and grow with the businesses



Core Values

• Be open, authentic, and communicate with clarity and integrity.

• Take passionate ownership—deliver what you promise, learn from mistakes, and bring solutions.

• Be resourceful, proactive, and committed to customer success.

• Work well with others—show empathy, respect time, and maintain humility with confidence.

• Bloom where you are planted—embrace growth, creativity, and pride in your work.

• Have fun and bring positive energy while striving for excellence.

 



How to Apply

Please submit your application through the form below. Only candidates who complete the form will be considered along with up to date resumes, including months + year to show total duration in a role/company. There will be a loom video exercise to show communication and understanding.

per month depending on total hours and experience

title: Content & Admin VA for a Maui-Based Women's Coaching Brand (CapCut + Canva)
description:
JOB TITLE

Content & Admin VA for a Maui-Based Women's Coaching Brand (CapCut + Canva)



 JOB TYPE

Part-time  ·  10–15 hours per week  ·  Long-term preferred



SALARY / RATE

$5 – $8 per hour (based on experience)

Weekly pay via Wise or PayPal  ·  Paid on time, every time



JOB OVERVIEW



Aloha! I'm a women's coach and content creator based in Maui, Hawaii. I'm building a TikTok + Pinterest content engine to grow my coaching practice, and I need a reliable, detail-oriented Content VA to help me bring it to life.



This is a **long-term role** for someone who's organized, creative, and genuinely excited about content that helps women heal, rebuild, and rise. You'll be the person turning my raw voice memos and scripts into finished TikTok videos, posting them, and keeping my content rhythm steady.



If you're looking for a kind, direct, communicative employer who pays on time and treats you like a teammate — let's talk.



WHAT YOU WILL DO (Your Core 5)



1. **Edit faceless voiceover TikToks** in CapCut from my raw audio + scripts (captions, b-roll, transitions, on-screen text, trending audio under voiceover)

2. **Design Pinterest pins in Canva** (using templates I'll provide) and post 3–5 per week

3. **Post and schedule** content on TikTok, Instagram, and Pinterest (native apps, no third-party schedulers)

4. **Light inbox + DM triage** (flag important messages, archive noise) + manage Calendly booking confirmations

5. **Maintain the content calendar** in a shared Google Doc or Notion page



REQUIRED SKILLS



- **CapCut** — confident with trimming, captions, transitions, b-roll overlays, text animations

- **Canva** — comfortable working from templates (no original graphic design needed)

- **Native posting** on TikTok, Instagram, and Pinterest

- **Clear written English** — able to write captions in my voice after I give you a style guide

- **Reliable internet** and a quiet workspace

- **At least 2 hours of timezone overlap** with Hawaii Standard Time (HST, UTC-10)

- **Detail-oriented and self-managed** — I do not micromanage



NICE TO HAVE (Not Required)



- Pinterest business account experience

- ChatGPT or Claude fluency for caption brainstorming

- Experience editing faceless voiceover content specifically

- Knowledge of coaching, wellness, or personal development niches



WHAT I OFFER



- **Long-term, stable work** (I'm looking for someone for 6+ months, not a one-off project)

- **Weekly pay, on time, every time** (via Wise or PayPal)

- **Kind and direct communication** — I give feedback clearly and respect your time

- **Room to grow** — as my business scales, your hours and pay will too

- **A clear workflow** — all tasks in ONE shared doc, one weekly check-in, no chaos across 5 apps

- **Warm, purposeful brand** — you'll be helping real women change their lives



 HOW TO APPLY



Please send me a message with the following **in this exact order** (I will not read applications that skip this):



1. **Subject line:** `Maui Coach VA — [Your First Name]`

2. **A short intro:** 3–4 sentences about who you are and why this role speaks to you (no templates, please)

3. **Your CapCut experience:** How long have you used it? What kind of videos have you edited?

4. **2–3 portfolio links** of TikToks or Reels you've edited (yours or for a client)

5. **Your weekly availability:** How many hours, and what are your usual working hours in HST?

6. **Your hourly rate** in USD

7. **This phrase at the bottom of your message so I know you read the whole post:** `I read the whole thing — let's build.`



THE PROCESS



- **Step 1:** I review applications and shortlist my top 5

- **Step 2:** 20-minute Zoom interview with the shortlist

- **Step 3:** **Paid 1-week trial** ($25–50 for a small real project) — I will never ask you for free work

- **Step 4:** I offer the role to the person who made my life easier in the trial



 WHAT NOT TO DO



- Please don't send a copy-paste template

- Please don't skip the application questions above

- Please don't apply if you can't commit to at least 10 hours per week consistently



ABOUT ME



I'm a single mom based in Maui, building a coaching practice rooted in honesty, emotional intelligence, and real transformation. My content is faceless voiceover + occasional face-to-camera, warm and grounded in tone. I'm looking for a teammate who cares about doing great work and wants to grow with a brand that's just getting started.



Mahalo for reading all the way through. I'm excited to meet you.



— Caroline

title: Regulatory Support Administrator
description:
We are an Australian-based fragrance and lifestyle business supplying products globally across home fragrance, personal care, and FMCG markets.



We are looking for a highly organised and detail-oriented Regulatory Support Administrator to assist with managing and issuing technical documentation required for product compliance.



This is a remote contract role, working with an international team and aligned to Australian business hours.



Key Responsibilities:

• Manage a shared regulatory inbox and respond to documentation requests.

• Prepare and issue regulatory documents including:

Safety Data Sheets (SDS)

IFRA Certificates

Allergen Declarations

Technical Data Sheets (TDS)

Certificates of Analysis (CoA)

• Maintain accurate document control, versioning, and filing systems.

• Track and manage product compliance documentation across multiple products.

• Liaise with internal teams (technical, sales, operations).

• Support regulatory workflows and process improvements.



Required Experience:

• 2+ years experience in administration, compliance, or technical documentation roles.

• Strong experience with document control and structured workflows.

• Excellent written English and communication skills.

• High attention to detail and accuracy.

• Ability to manage multiple requests and deadlines.



Highly Regarded (Not Essential):

• Experience with SDS, IFRA, or regulatory documentation.

• Background in cosmetics, fragrance, chemical, or FMCG industries.



Working Structure:

• Remote contract role.

• Must be available during Australian Eastern Time (AEST) business hours.

• Initial 3-month contract to assess fit, with strong potential for ongoing work for the right candidate.



Application Instructions:

To apply, please follow these instructions carefully:

1. Begin your application with:

"Regulatory Support Pro"

2. Include the following:

• A link to your resume.

• Your relevant experience with documentation, compliance, or admin roles.

• Any experience with SDS, IFRA, or regulatory documentation (if applicable).

• Your availability in AEST hours.

• A short example of a process or system you have managed or improved.



Applications that do not follow these instructions will not be reviewed.

title: Subject: High-Energy Growth Partner | Expert at Scaling AI SaaS for Contractors
description:
The Mission

Most roofing contractors are drowning in paperwork. CompanySync changes that with a 60-second AI satellite estimator. We aren't looking for "workers"; we are looking for Growth Partners who can command a conversation with American business owners and show them the future of their industry.



Why This is Different

The Tech: You aren't selling "leads." You’re demoing a cutting-edge AI (Lexi) that actually solves a contractor's biggest headache.



The Pay: This is built for earners. While there is a weekly retainer, the real money is in the $75 per subscriber conversion. If you can close, you will make more here in 10 hours than most make in 40.



The Autonomy: No rigid scripts. You need to be smart enough to handle objections on the fly and "vibe" with blue-collar owners.



Who You Are

Elite Communication: Your English is near-native/neutral. You can hold a professional, high-energy conversation without sounding like a telemarketer.



Tech-Fluent: You can navigate a CRM, record personalized Loom videos, and run a smooth Zoom demo without breaking a sweat.



The "Shine": You have a personality that cuts through the noise. When you send a video to a lead, they have to click it.



Compensation Structure (The "Hustler" Model)

$100/Week Retainer: Paid for meeting baseline outreach volume.



$15 Per Qualified Trial: Every time a user runs an AI estimate.



$75 Per Paid Conversion: When that trial turns into a monthly subscriber.



? MANDATORY APPLICATION STEP

Do not just click apply. We only hire people who can sell.

Send us a 1-minute video (Loom or YouTube).



Briefly pitch yourself.



Tell us why you're the right person to sell AI to a skeptical roofing contractor.

Applications without a video link will be instantly deleted.Applications without a video will be ignored.

Our team is growing quickly and looking for our next project coordinator to help us maintain our quality and turnaround standards.

Communicate with Admin team on deliveries

Extreme attention to detail.

Excellent written English.

Proficient in Canva, Adobe Photoshop, Adobe Lightroom, Slack.

Fast learner and willing to learn

Tech-savvy - comfortable with quickly learning new software

Excellent internet connection (this job is a fully remote position and you will be uploading large files)

Very close attention to detail

Real estate editing experience is a big pro

Graphic design experience is a pro

Basic video editing skills is a pro 



INSTRUCTIONS TO APPLY



Please
 
 a video recording of you answering the following questions:

Thank you!

ntum

title: Wedding Video Editor – Premiere Pro
description:
Looking for a Wedding Editor Who Actually Gets It

Hey — I'm Justin, co-owner of ToneMedia, a wedding videography company based in New Jersey, USA. We've been shooting weddings for 12 years across some of the top venues in the NY/NJ area.

I'm not looking for someone who "knows Premiere Pro." I'm looking for someone who watches back a first dance and knows exactly where to cut. Someone who hears the toast building and already feels where the music should breathe. If you've edited weddings before, you know what I mean.

Here's what this is:

We need a Premiere Pro editor for wedding highlight reels, ceremony edits, and reception content. This starts as project-based with real potential to become ongoing and consistent work as our season ramps up.



What I need from you to apply:



2-3 wedding edits you're proud of (links, not screenshots)

Your rate — per project, per finished minute, or hourly. Be honest. I respect transparency and I pay fairly.

Your current availability and typical turnaround

Confirmation you can complete a paid test edit within 2-3 days of receiving footage



What you'll get from us:



-Organized footage, clear creative direction, and a reference cut so you're never guessing

-Respectful communication and timely payment. Always.

-A real working relationship, not a one-off gig where you never hear from us again



Please don't apply if:



You edit on DaVinci, Final Cut, or anything other than Premiere Pro

You've never edited a full wedding before

You can't turn around the test edit in 2-3 days



If you've read this far and you're thinking "this sounds like someone I'd actually want to work with"  that's the point. Send me a message with your samples, rate, and availability.



Talk soon.

— Justin "tone"

4. Admin & Organization:

     - Track project deadlines, approvals, and outstanding tasks.

     - Monitor
s and respond or route them appropriately.



What We’re Looking For

title: Voice Production Specialist — YouTube AI Voiceover
description:
Position Overview



We produce educational YouTube videos (10–11 minutes each) for a relationship psychology channel. All voiceover is generated using ElevenLabs AI with a custom voice. We need someone to take our finished scripts and produce broadcast-ready audio files for every video — from generation through final cleanup — delivered directly to our Google Drive.



What you'll receive per video: A clean script with ElevenLabs audio tags already embedded, workspace access to our custom voice and settings, and a Drive folder ready to receive files.



What you'll deliver per video: 1 individual audio section files, generated and named per our convention, normalized to -14 LUFS, light compression applied, exported as MP3 44.1kHz 128kbps and uploaded to the correct Drive subfolder.



ElevenLabs specs: eleven_v3 model · Stability 0.50 · Similarity 0.80 · Speaker Boost off · Audio tags pre-written in the script.



Audio post specs: -14 LUFS per section · High-pass filter · De-essing if needed · Flat or off-tone sections regenerated before delivery.



Backlog: ~28 existing videos need post-processing cleanup only (no regeneration). This is your first priority on hire.

Ongoing volume: 4-6/week to start, scaling within 3 months



Payment:



Backlog rate is $4–6/video. Once work effectiveness is proven by the completion of the backlog, we can negotiate a higher salary .

title: Customer Service Representative – Workers Comp Drug and Alcohol Testing
description:
CUSTOMER SERVICE REPRESENTATIVE – WORKERS’ COMPENSATION 

DRUG & ALCOHOL TESTING (REMOTE – PHILIPPINES) 

*LOCATION: METRO MANILA & CAVITE APPLICANTS ONLY 



=== ABOUT THE ROLE === 

USA Mobile Drug Testing (USAMDT) is hiring a Customer Service Representative (CSR) to support our Workers’ Compensation Drug and Alcohol Testing operations. 



This role works closely with our Workers’ Compensation and Claims teams to process testing requests, coordinate collections, and obtain results as quickly and accurately as possible. Speed, attention to detail, and clear communication are critical. 



This is a specialized CSR position, not a script-based call center role. You will handle live calls, time-sensitive injury-related requests, documentation, and follow-up. 



We operate in a process-driven, accountability-focused environment and are organizing the company around the EOS / Traction framework, emphasizing role clarity, ownership, and disciplined execution. 



This is a long-term role with structured training, scorecards, and defined milestones. 



=== KEY RESPONSIBILITIES === 

PHONE &
 COMMUNICATION 

• Answer inbound calls live and professionally 

• Return voi
s within 30 minutes 

• Respond to
s within 4 hours 

• Interpret unclear or rambling explanations related to injuries or claims 

• Ask clarifying questions to ensure correct testing is ordered 

• Communicate expectations, timelines, and next steps clearly 

• Log all calls and interactions accurately 

• Escalate urgent or high-risk situations immediately 



WORKERS’ COMPENSATION TESTING COORDINATION 

• Process workers’ compensation drug and alcohol testing requests 

• Coordinate with employers, claims teams, collectors, clinics, and labs 

• Schedule collections quickly and accurately 

• Confirm availability before finalizing schedules 

• Prioritize cases based on urgency and client requirements 

• Maintain accurate job and claim status codes 



RESULTS TRACKING & DOCUMENTATION 

• Track collections and follow up on incomplete or missing information 

• Monitor test progress and obtain results as quickly as possible 

• Process paperwork from collectors and clinics 

• Edit and finalize documents using Foxit PDF 

• Upload documents to SharePoint or route results internally 

• Update job logs with donor information, test stages, and claim status 

• Ensure documentation accuracy and compliance 



CUSTOMER & INTERNAL SUPPORT 

• Provide status updates to employers and internal teams 

• Assist with claims-related questions and next-step clarification 

• Maintain professionalism during sensitive or stressful calls 



=== REQUIRED EXPERIENCE AND QUALIFICATIONS === 

• Minimum 3 years supporting a small U.S.-based company 

• At least 3 years of live voice experience with U.S. customers 

• Strong verbal and written English at a professional level 

• Ability to manage time-sensitive requests in a fast-paced environment 

• Experience with Microsoft Office, SharePoint, and CRM tools 

• Comfort following documented workflows and escalation paths 

• High attention to detail and personal accountability 



=== CRITICAL CHARACTERISTICS FOR SUCCESS === 

The ideal candidate consistently demonstrates: 

• Critical thinking to interpret unclear or rambling explanations 

• Intellectual humility — asks questions and seeks clarity 

• Proactive behavior without waiting for direction 

• Strong sense of urgency, especially for injury-related cases 

• Attention to detail and follow-through 

• Effective priority management and time management 

• Punctuality and reliability 

• Integrity — doing what you said you would do, when you said you would do it, and cleaning it up when you don’t 

• Clear, concise communication that directly addresses the issue at hand 



=== ENGLISH COMMUNICATION REQUIREMENT (NON-NEGOTIABLE) === 

This role requires advanced English communication to support U.S.-based employers and internal claims teams. 



You must be able to: 

• Clearly understand American callers in real time 

• Communicate confidently without scripts 

• Handle urgent or sensitive conversations professionally 



--- USA ENGLISH LIVE CONVERSATION ASSESSMENT --- 

• Shortlisted candidates will complete a real-time English conversation assessment 

• Live voice or video format 

• Scenario-based discussion 

• Accent is not evaluated — clarity, comprehension, and professionalism are 



=== PERSONALITY & WORK-STYLE ASSESSMENT === 

Candidates may be asked to complete a personality or work-style assessment to help evaluate communication style, accountability, and role fit. Results are reviewed alongside interviews and performance indicators and are not used as standalone pass/fail criteria. 



=== POWER, INTERNET & EQUIPMENT REQUIREMENTS (NON-NEGOTIABLE) === 

POWER BACKUP 

• Within 90 days, you must have 7–8 hours of reliable backup power 

• Must support a laptop or desktop, second monitor, and modem/router 

• Stand-alone battery solution required (multiple units acceptable) 

• Photos and specifications must be provided 

*** FAILURE TO MEET THIS REQUIREMENT WITHIN 90 DAYS WILL RESULT IN TERMINATION *** 



HEADSET REQUIREMENT 

• Professional noise-cancelling headset suitable for U.S. phone support 

• Clear microphone quality with minimal background noise 

• Approved headset options provided during onboarding 



=== TRAINING, PERFORMANCE & CAREER PROGRESSION === 

INITIAL TRAINING (0–90 DAYS) 

• Workers’ Compensation testing workflows 

• Documentation standards and quality expectations 

• Performance scorecards and accountability metrics 



CSR LEVEL 1 

• Achieved at 90 days with performance score of 4 out of 5 or higher 



CSR LEVEL 2 

• Earned through consistent performance and skill mastery 

• Handles more complex cases with reduced supervision 



This role does not include secondary roles. Focus is on Workers’ Compensation testing accuracy and speed. 



=== COMPENSATION === 

• Compensation range: $4.00 to $7.00 USD per hour 

• $4.00 USD per hour during training 

• Raises at 90 days, 6 months, 9 months, and 12 months (performance-based) 

• Additional incentives tied to KPIs and role proficiency 

 

=== TIME OFF POLICY === 

• First 90 days: No time off 

• Remainder of first year: Up to 5 unpaid days 

• After one year: 5 paid days, usable in 2-hour increments 

Candidates will be asked to disclose any planned time off for the next 12 months during the interview. 



=== IMPORTANT NOTES === 

• Applicants must currently reside in Metro Manila or Cavite 

• This role requires stability, reliability, and long-term commitment

Your automations help the company scale cleanly.

, Zapier, and n8n

 triggers)

REQUIREMENTS (MUST-HAVE)

- Advanced 
 experience

- Experience with Slack automations and integrations

- Experience with Zapier

- Strong understanding of APIs and webhooks

STRONG PLUS

WHY JOIN US?

title: GoHighLevel (GHL) Customer Support & Onboarding Specialist (Full-Time, Remote)
description:
We’re looking for a highly experienced GoHighLevel (GHL) specialist to support our customers through onboarding, technical support, and ongoing platform usage.



This is a hands-on role combining customer support + technical execution, so you should be just as comfortable troubleshooting inside GHL as you are communicating clearly and confidently with clients.



You will play a key role in ensuring our clients have a smooth, high-quality experience using the platform.





What You’ll Be Doing



Customer Support & Communication

• Respond to customer questions via
, chat, and support tickets

• Guide customers step-by-step to help them understand and use the platform

• Maintain clear, friendly, and professional communication at all times

• Support customers inside our community and ongoing support channels



Onboarding & Calls

• Lead onboarding calls (1:1 and group) via Zoom

• Walk customers through setup and best practices

• Host support calls and troubleshoot issues live



Technical Support (GoHighLevel)

• Troubleshoot issues across:

• Funnels

• Workflows & automations

• Pipelines

• Calendars

• Domains & integrations

• Test setups to ensure everything is working correctly

• Identify and fix errors in workflows, triggers, and automations

• Use internal resources and GHL support before escalating issues



Systems & Process Improvement

• Keep support conversations organized and documented

• Identify recurring issues and help improve support systems

• Communicate clearly with internal tea
mbers on technical updates





What You Must Be Able To Do (Inside GHL)



You should already be confident doing the following:

• Set up and troubleshoot workflows and automations

• Build and edit funnels and landing pages

• Set up and manage pipelines and opportunity stages

• Configure calendars and booking systems

• Connect and troubleshoot domains and DNS settings

• Set up
 and SMS systems (Mailgun, LC
, A2P basics)

• Import and manage contacts and custom fields

• Connect and troubleshoot Stripe / payment integrations

• Handle subscription management (pausing, canceling, refunds)

• Diagnose and fix workflow errors and broken automations





What We’re Looking For

• Strong, hands-on experience with GoHighLevel (this is required)

• Comfortable leading Zoom calls and explaining technical concepts clearly

• Strong written and spoken English

• Ability to troubleshoot independently and find solutions quickly

• Clear, calm, and professional communication style

• Comfortable working with non-technical customers

• Highly organized, reliable, and detail-oriented

• Takes initiative and uses available resources before asking for help





Work Schedule

• Full-time (30–40 hours per week)

• Must be available during Eastern Time (ET) business hours

• Must be consistent and reliable for real-time collaboration



- Must have excellent spoken and written English.

- Clear, strong, reliable internet connection with quiet and professional working environment for frequent Zoom calls.

- Must be willing to be on camera, and present professionally.



What Success Looks Like

• Customers feel supported, confident, and taken care of

• Onboarding calls are clear, smooth, and effective

• Support requests are handled quickly and professionally

• Technical issues are resolved efficiently

• Systems are set up correctly and consistently

• You become a trusted point of contact for both customers and team



This Role Is NOT For You If…

• You need step-by-step guidance for basic GHL tasks

• You rely on others to troubleshoot issues for you

• You are not comfortable speaking on Zoom with customers

• You are inconsistent with availability or communication

• You get overwhelmed handling multiple conversations or tickets





How to Apply



Please include:

• A short introduction about your experience with GoHighLevel

• Examples of onboarding or customer support experience

• Examples of automations or systems you’ve built or supported

• Confirmation of your availability during Eastern Time hours

• Optional (recommended): a short video introduction





About Firestarter Funnels



Firestarter Funnels is part of the Firestarter ecosystem, founded by entrepreneur Willa Kammerer.



We support entrepreneurs, coaches, and business owners in building high-caliber personal brands and scalable online businesses through strategy, systems, and execution.



Our platform is a white-labeled version of GoHighLevel, designed specifically for personal brand businesses — combining funnels, websites, CRM, automations, and
 marketing into one integrated system, supported by hands-on guidance.

title: Virtual Assistant for Events & Digital Brands
description:
Pesos
/ per hour (Hours depending on project needs) Roughly 20 hours per week



We’re building some of the most recognised brands in the short term rental and accommodation space across Australia and New Zealand. That means conferences, podcasts, digital platforms, websites, content, and a lot of moving parts.



Right now, we’re looking for a self employed contractor who wants more than a standard VA gig. This is a hands on, fast paced support role sitting at the heart of three growing brands:



* STRIVE Conference

Industry leading events and awards bringing together property managers, owners, and suppliers across Australia, New Zealand, and beyond.



* The Accommodation Show

A long running podcast featuring operators, founders, and leaders in hospitality, travel, and short term rentals.



* iBooked.online

A digital agency focused on direct bookings, websites, SEO, and marketing strategy for accommodation businesses.



If you enjoy variety, problem solving, and being close to the action, you’ll feel right at home.



What You’ll Actually Be Doing



This role is deliberately broad. You’ll touch a lot of areas, without being expected to be an expert in all of them from day one.



Admin and operations

* Managing inboxes and basic diary coordination

* Organising data, lists, and spreadsheets

* Supporting day to day business admin across the brands



Content and marketing support

* Scheduling social media posts across multiple platforms

* Assisting with podcast uploads, episode assets, and show notes

* Helping prepare
 campaigns and announcements

* Light content editing and formatting



Web and tech support

* Simple WordPress edits and page updates

* Uploading content, images, and basic changes

* Working with tools like Google Workspace, social schedulers, and
 platforms



Events support

* Helping coordinate speakers, sponsors, and partners

* Keeping track of assets, deadlines, and comms

* Supporting event logistics before, during, and after delivery



You won’t just be following instructions. You’ll be encouraged to spot gaps, suggest improvements, and contribute ideas for content, campaigns, and processes.



Hours, Flexibility & Setup



* Around 20 to 60 hours per week

* Spread across 5 to 6 days

* Fully remote and flexible

* Work to your own rhythm, as long as things get done



This suits freelancers who like consistent work without being locked into rigid hours.



Who This Is Great For



This role is ideal if you:



* Are a freelancer or contractor looking for a long term client

* Enjoy variety rather than doing the same task all day

* Like hospitality, travel, marketing, media, or tech

* Want exposure to events and the short term rental industry

* Are organised, detail oriented, and proactive



You’ll be working with people and brands across Australia and New Zealand, and you’ll build genuine industry connections along the way.



Skills We’re Looking For



* Strong written English

* Confidence with Google Workspace

* Comfortable with social media tools

* Basic WordPress experience

* Good internet connection and a solid home setup

* Curious mindset and willingness to learn



You don’t need to know everything upfront. You do need to be reliable, switched on, and comfortable juggling priorities.



Growth & Pay



As your role expands and your impact grows, your rates will grow too. This is a relationship we want to invest in, not a short term stop gap.



How to Apply



Send us:



* A short intro about you

* A link to something you’ve worked on that you’re proud of

* Your hourly or project based rate



If you like being close to the action and want to grow alongside brands that are genuinely building something in this space, we’d love to hear from you.

title: Are You The SEO Specialist With Killer Skills, & Real Results
description:
StressFree FrontDesk is hiring an SEO specialist (technical + content/on-page) to improve search visibility and rankings across multiple client websites.



What You’ll Do

Audit each site and build a prioritized SEO roadmap (quick wins + longer-term growth).

Resolve technical SEO issues that block performance (crawl/indexing problems, 404/4XX errors, broken links, redirects).

Improve on-page SEO at scale (titles, meta descriptions, headings, internal linking, content structure).

Identify keyword opportunities and map them to the right pages (service pages, location pages, collections/categories, blogs).

Recommend and/or create SEO content improvements (expand thin pages, add FAQs, strengthen service pages).

Improve local SEO where relevant (Google Business Profile recommendations, location targeting, citations guidance).

Monitor results and report progress (rankings, traffic trends, leads where available).

Document changes clearly so work is repeatable across multiple sites.



Qualifications

Proven experience improving rankings through technical SEO + on-page/content SEO.

Strong understanding of crawling/indexing, redirects, canonicalization, and site architecture.

Ability to do keyword research and translate it into page-level actions.

Comfortable working across different website platforms/CMS environments.

Clear communicator with strong documentation habits.



Nice to Have

Experience with local SEO for service businesses.

Experience with eCommerce/catalog-style sites.



To Apply

This role supports multiple client websites. You should be comfortable switching contexts, keeping work organized per client, and documenting changes clearly.

 your resume to 
 and answer these 3 questions (applications without links/screenshots/examples will not be reviewed):



1. SEO Experience Roadmap: How long have you been doing SEO? Provide a timeline in this format for your last 2 clients:

Client/Company name (or industry if under NDA)

Dates (month/year – month/year)

Role & weekly responsibilities (technical SEO, on-page, content, local SEO, link building, reporting)

Platforms/CMS used (WordPress, Shopify, Wix, custom, etc.)

Tools used (GSC, GA4, Ahrefs/Semrush/Ubersuggest, Screaming Frog, etc.)

1 measurable outcome (rankings/traffic/leads/conversions — numbers preferred)



2. “Receipts” + Improvements: Share 3 examples of SEO work you’ve done (links, screenshots, or short Loom videos). For each example include:

Site type + goal (local service / ecom / lead gen)

What you were responsible for (audit, redirects, internal linking, content updates, GBP, etc.)

What you changed (be specific: titles/meta, content sections, redirect map, fixing 404s, pruning/noindex, etc.)

Before ? after results (rankings/traffic/leads) and timeframe

One process improvement you introduced (templates, SOPs, reporting cadence, QA checklist) and what it changed (time saved, fewer errors, faster turnaround)



3. Technical Depth Check

Redirects & 404s: Explain your process for handling 404/4XX errors on a live site. Include:

How you d
 restore vs 301 redirect vs 410

How you prevent redirect chains/loops

How you QA changes (what you check in GSC/crawls)

One example where your approach improved crawl/indexing

On-page at scale: Explain how you fix duplicate titles/meta descriptions across hundreds of pages without doing them one-by-one. Include:

Your approach to templates/rules

How you handle faceted/pagination pages (canonical/noindex)

How you measure success (CTR, index coverage, rankings)

 Requirements

title: Recruiting Specialists
description:
Qualifications

2+ years of Staffing/Recruitment experience, strong understanding and ability to recruit in all levels of the organization

Excellent inter-personal skills, communication skills and team-based project experience

Clear understanding of the end-to-end recruitment life-cycle processes

Must have experience on mapping and sourcing resumes from all sources

Proficient computer skills including Microsoft Office applications and in-depth knowledge of data mining, boolean queries, internet research, and resume scraping

Strong attention to detail and willingness to learn

Strong command of verbal and written English



Responsibilities

As a Virtual Recruitment Specialist, you will be responsible for sourcing and screening applicants for a US based Home Health Agency, interviewing for hourly and salaried positions, and assisting in job fairs and other outreach events.



The ideal candidate will have considerable skills in interviewing techniques, a good knowledge of all clerical and specialized functions related to Human Resources and will have a good command of the English language. The candidate will effectively communicate with others in a friendly but professional manner.



Additional Responsibilities

Partner with Organization Leaders to forecast and develop plans for key staffing initiatives

Create and implement Employment Marketing Strategies to attract passive job seekers

Work with hiring managers and interview teams to ensure job requirements and expectations are clearly understood and candidates are assessed against appropriate criteria (Calibration)

Develop strong relationships and partner with hiring managers, business leaders and HR

Track and report key metrics designed to measure and predict staffing activity

Regular follow up with the respective hiring managers and candidates to ensure timeliness of recruitment process

Develop and implement search strategy that includes use of organizational approved tools and resources

title: Digital Marketing Virtual Assistant – Lead Generation Specialist (Insurance Agency)
description:
Blue Insurance Group is a fast-growing, community-driven insurance agency based in Florida. We are looking for a results-driven Digital Marketing Virtual Assistant who can create, manage, and optimize campaigns that generate qualified leads.



Our focus is simple: turn marketing into revenue.



This role is for someone who understands how to:



Build campaigns

Generate leads

Track performance

Improve results



If you only know how to “post content,” this role is not for you.



Key Responsibilities



Lead Generation Campaigns



Create and manage
 & Instagram ad campaigns

Build and optimize Google Ads campaigns (Search & Display)

Generate inbound leads for:

Homeowners insurance

Auto insurance

Life insurance



Set up and manage retargeting campaigns

Funnel & Conversion Optimization

Build and optimize landing pages (or work with templates provided)

Improve conversion rates (CPL, CTR, lead quality)

Track performance using analytics tools

A/B test creatives, headlines, and offers



 CRM & Automation Support

Integrate leads into CRM (QQ Catalyst or similar)

Set up basic
/SMS follow-up sequences

Ensure leads are properly tracked and followed up



Content & Creative Direction

Write ad copy that converts (not just sounds good)

Suggest and help create ad creatives (images/videos)

Align messaging with our brand voice:

“Making Insurance Make Sense”



Requirements



 Must-Have Skills

Proven experience running paid ads 
 & Google)

Strong understanding of lead generation funnels

Ability to read and analyze campaign data

Experience with landing pages and conversion optimization

Strong English communication skills



 Preferred Experience

Experience in insurance, real estate, or local service businesses

Familiarity with:

GoHighLevel, ClickFunnels, or similar

QQ Catalyst or other CRMs

Experience generating U.S.-based leads



 KPIs (How You’ll Be Measured)

Cost Per Lead (CPL)

Lead Quality (conversion to quotes/sales)

Number of qualified leads generated

Campaign ROI



Work Schedule

Compensation

Competitive base pay (based on experience)

What We’re Looking For (Culture Fit)



At Blue Insurance Group, we want someone who:



Thinks like a marketer AND a business owner

Is proactive (brings ideas, not just tasks)

 Understands urgency and speed

ing, and hitting up social media platforms

title: Campaign Strategist
description:
Remote | Full-time | Competitive base + performance bonus

We build and run cold outbound systems for B2B companies. Full chain: strategy, messaging, infrastructure, lead sourcing, execution, reply handling. We're scaling fast and need someone to own the client strategy layer and make sure everything moves. This isn't just a job. If you're the right person, this is a growth partner opportunity inside a company that's adding clients every single day.

The role



You're the client's main point of contact. You take their calls, understand their business, make the campaign decisions, and make sure the messaging is sharp before it goes live. Once campaigns are running, you monitor performance and make sure nothing stalls. You direct the teams underneath you, not by doing their work, but by making sure they follow through on theirs. If something is stuck, you unstick it. If a campaign is underperforming, you flag it and adjust.

What you do



Take onboarding and strategy calls with clients. Understand their ICP, how they sell, what makes them different, and how they want to sound. Configure campaigns by choosing the right approach for each client. Review and QA all messaging before it goes out. Get client sign-off on their campaigns. Hand off clean specs to the execution team. Monitor live campaigns and flag what needs attention. Direct the infrastructure, implementation, and VA teams to make sure deliverables are completed on time. Chase anything that's idle. Be the person who makes sure every client is moving through the pipeline and nothing falls through the cracks.



What you don't do

Set up domains,
 accounts, LinkedIn accounts, or any infrastructure yourself. Build anything in any tool yourself. Write code. Handle lead sourcing or data. The teams and systems do all of that. You make sure it gets done.

Who you are



Young, hungry, and experienced. You've already done this, you've run cold outbound at an agency, as a freelancer, or in-house, and you're looking for something bigger. You know what good cold messaging looks like and what gets ignored. You can sit on a call with a business owner and walk away understanding exactly what they need. You have opinions about outbound. You're fast, direct, and you don't let things sit. You're the person who checks the dashboard, sees something at zero, and chases the right person until it's not at zero anymore.



Experience with Clay, n8n, or working at an outbound agency is a strong plus. US/UK based preferred.

Bonus if you play strategy games. Civ, HOI4, EU4, map games, anything where you're thinking about resource allocation, positioning, and long-term moves. This role is the same thing. Pure strategy, no grunt work. If you enjoy those games, you'll enjoy this.



We're not hiring someone who wants to clock in and clock out. We're looking for someone who sees where this is going and wants to grow with it. The right person in this role will be running a significant part of this operation within months.

What you get



You own the strategy and client relationship layer of a rapidly scaling outbound operation. No more setting up inboxes, warming domains, building Clay tables, or doing implementation grunt work. Strategy, messaging, monitoring, and making sure the machine keeps moving. Competitive base plus performance comp tied to client throughput. Remote. Flexible. Results-driven. And if you're the right person, a real seat at the table as we scale.



How to apply

Send your CV and a Loom under 3 minutes. Subject line: "Campaign Strategist, [Your Name]"

In the Loom, walk me through the last cold outbound campaign you ran. Who was the target, what messaging did you use, what happened.

title: Medical Billing Auditor (Denied Claims & Revenue Recovery)
description:
We are looking for an experienced medical billing auditor / AR specialist who can identify missed revenue, undercoding, and denied claims issues. Ideally with experience in procedural/surgical specialties (orthopedics/cardiology/neurosurgery/anesthesiology/etc) and dentistry.



This is NOT a basic claim submission role. We are specifically looking for someone who can analyze claims and explain why revenue was lost and how to recover it.



Responsibilities:

Review medical claims and identify missed CPT codes, modifiers, or documentation gaps

Analyze denied or underpaid claims and determine root cause

Recommend corrections or resubmission strategies

Provide clear explanations of findings

Preferred Experience:

Strong experience with denial management / AR follow-ups

Knowledge of CPT coding, modifiers (25, 59, etc.), and bundling rules

Experience with US healthcare billing (Medicare, commercial payers)

Familiarity with systems like Epic, eClinicalWorks, or payer portals

Important:



We are NOT looking for someone who only submits claims.

We are looking for someone who can think critically and find missed money in claims.



If you have experience identifying underpayments or improving reimbursement, we would love to speak with you.

title: Outbound Customer Insights Specialist (Telemarketing + User Research + Remote + US time)
description:
Fully Remote (must be available during US business hours, typically 8 AM – 5 PM EST / flexible within US time zones to align with target users)



Note: Upload a video to Google Drive to give an introduction about yourself and why you want to apply to this position.



* About the Role



We are looking for a sharp, proactive, and AI-savvy professional who thrives on reaching out to people, uncovering real problems, and turning those conversations into product-changing insights. This is not a traditional telemarketing or customer support role. You will run high-volume outbound calls and
s to US-based target users, dig deep into their daily workflows, and surface pain points that directly shape how our product is built. 



- Your critical thinking, observations, and recommendations will regularly influence the product roadmap.

- You will be the voice of the customer inside the company — someone who combines excellent outreach skills with strong analytical ability and fluency in AI tools to turn raw conversations into clear, actionable intelligence for the product team.



* Key Responsibilities



- Execute daily outbound calling and
ing campaigns to reach a high volume of target users in the US.

- Lead structured discovery conversations (phone +
) to identify workflow challenges, frustrations, and unmet needs.

- Document findings clearly and synthesize insights into concise reports, themes, and recommendations for the product, engineering, and design teams.

- Actively contribute ideas and suggestions that help shape product features and priorities.

- Use AI tools creatively and efficiently (e.g., for call/email personalization, conversation summarization, sentiment analysis, pattern detection, research, and follow-up automation).

- Track all interactions accurately.

- Meet weekly outreach and insight-quality targets while maintaining a professional, helpful, and engaging tone.

- Stay curious and continuously refine your approach based on what you learn from users.



* Requirements



- Strong critical thinking and analytical skills — you naturally spot patterns, ask great follow-up questions, and connect the dots between individual stories and broader product opportunities.

- Excellent verbal and written communication skills with the ability to quickly build rapport over the phone and in
.

- Proven experience in outbound calling, telemarketing, sales development, customer support, or user research (2+ years preferred).

- Fluent with AI tools at a practical level (you already use tools like ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, or similar for research, writing, summarization, or automation and can learn new ones quickly).

- Highly self-motivated and comfortable working independently in a remote environment.

- Available and productive during US business hours.

- Passion for understanding how people work and a genuine desire to make products better through real user feedback.

- No specific degree required — we care about your ability to think, communicate, and deliver insights.



* Application

- In your, tell us about the amazing things you have done. 

- Show us your work ethnics. Tell us about how you work and what you would do to make your team respect and trust you.

title: SEO Specialist (On-Page & Off-Page) – Watch Brand (Remote)

This is a **remote role** and we are looking for someone who can take ownership of the SEO strategy rather than simply follow instructions.



---



**Key Responsibilities**



• Conduct keyword research relevant to watches, fashion accessories, and lifestyle products

• Optimize website pages for SEO (titles, meta descriptions, headings, internal linking)

• Improve product page and collection page SEO

• Identify opportunities to rank for high-value search terms

• Work on improving the technical SEO health of the website

• Identify blog topics that can rank on Google and bring organic traffic

• Track SEO performance and recommend improvements

---



**Blog Responsibilities**



You will also help guide our **in-house blog strategy**, including:



• Keyword research for blog articles

• SEO optimization for blog posts

• Suggesting article topics that attract organic search traffic

• Ensuring blog content supports SEO growth



---



**Requirements**

• Strong understanding of **on-page SEO and off-page SEO**

• Experience with keyword research tools

• Understanding of technical SEO fundamentals

title: Gaming YouTube Script Writer (HSR, ZZZ, JRPGs)
description:
A dream job for a gamer who loves to write!



I run a growing YouTube channel focused on gacha games and JRPGs. I’m looking for a script writer who genuinely loves gaming and can turn messy, complex game info (kits, lore, story beats, community drama, patch changes) into scripts that are easy to follow, entertaining and accurate.



YOU WILL BE WRITING ACROSS:

- Zenless Zone Zero (required deep knowledge)

- Honkai: Star Rail (required deep knowledge)

- JRPGs and other upcoming games (example: a plot summary for a Dragon Quest remaster)

- Future games (example: Ananta)



One week you might write a ZZZ character lore breakdown and a “push or skip” video for Honkai Star Rail. The next week you might write a story summary or “what you need to know” video for a completely different game.



WHAT YOU'LL DO:

Write YouTube scripts for:



- Character guides, lore explainers, “pull or skip”, patch overviews and other stuff that audiences might like

- Plot summaries and recaps (spoiler-safe when required)

- Short-form scripts (Shorts/Reels style)

- Take complicated mechanics or lore and make them simple for everyday viewers

- Match different tones depending on the audience and video goal (funny, serious, hype, explanatory, story-time)

- Suggest video opportunities based on what’s trending in the games we cover (patch cycles, banners, drama, community interest, search demand)



REQUIRED:

- No agency submissions - I’m looking for individuals who want to be part of a team yet enjoy working from home

- No AI-written scripts: when it comes to game guides, AI is often inaccurate and misses nuance. I’m hiring a human writer for a reason.

- Strong existing knowledge of Zenless Zone Zero and Honkai: Star Rail, including characters and lore (you will be asked specific questions to confirm this)

- Adaptable to learning new games fast

- Able to write clearly and logically, with strong pacing and hooks

- Fast delivery without sacrificing quality

- A PC, mobile device or console capable of playing the games we cover (ZZZ, HSR, and upcoming titles). Must be able to easily download and play new games that may need coverage.



NICE TO HAVE:

- A sense of humour 

- You already watch gaming YouTube and understand retention, hooks, pacing and “why people keep watching”

- You write and read A LOT

- You can pitch strong titles hooks, and thumbnail text options



PAY STRUCTURE (PATHWAY):

This role starts as per-script, with clear upside for great work.



Step 1: 8-week paid trial (pay-per-script)



- Pay per script based on length/format

- Clear deadlines agreed before each script

- Must be open to revisions and feedback (especially the first few weeks to get the tone of voice right with the YouTube channel)



Step 2: If we work well together



- We move to a small monthly retainer for a set number of scripts (still part-time), with extra scripts priced at the same per-script rate.



To apply: include your preferred rates for:



1. Your per-script rate (in PHP) for each format:



3–5 min script: $___

6–10 min script: $___

10–30 min script: $___



2. Your preferred monthly retainer after the trial (and what it includes):



“$___/month for ___ scripts” (tell me the mix and revision limits)





HOW TO APPLY



Send:

 1. A short intro about you and your gaming background

 2. Your writing samples (scripts preferred, otherwise any strong writing)

 3. Your rates per script length (listed above)

 4. Your weekly availability and typical turnaround time per script

 5. Confirm what device you use to play ZZZ/HSR (PC/mobile/console)

words only, explain how Honkai Star Rail’s Cyrene works to a brand new player.

title: CAREER OPPORTUNITY »» Content Quality Analyst ¤ Full Time ¤
description:
We’re hiring a Content Quality Analyst to help ensure the quality and consistency of web content across our firm’s website. This junior-level role is well-suited for someone who’s detail-oriented, organized, and comfortable working within legal subject matters. You should be confident reviewing content with a critical eye, open to learning, and willing to speak up when you spot issues or areas for improvement. You’ll also work with other teams to keep everything consistent and up to standard across the site. A legal background isn’t required, but the ability to understand legal concepts and apply established guidelines accurately is essential.



Ready to Apply?

Send your resume to 



Key Responsibilities:

-Review web content for legal marketing compliance, factual and conceptual accuracy, grammar, and readability.

-Conduct regular audits of web pages for both content and technical issues.

-Ensure SEO best practices are implemented throughout all content (keywords, meta tags, headers, etc.).

-Perform basic technical checks (e.g., internal links, alt text, metadata, formatting).

-Coordinate with different teams to ensure consistent application of content standards.

-Track content updates and maintain documentation of reviews and recommendations.

-Support the development of internal content standards and style guides.



Qualifications And Skills:

-Excellent attention to detail and the ability to identify content inconsistencies or errors.

-Strong organizational and time management skills.

-Ability to follow complex editorial, legal, and brand standards accurately.

-Fast learner with the ability to adapt to legal content and digital platforms.

-Strong written communication and analytical skills.

-Familiarity with legal terminology, especially in the U.S. legal landscape (preferred).

-Understanding of basic website development concepts such as HTML tags and page structure (preferred).



Basic Requirements:

-Device Specifications: Intel i5 processor or higher with a minimum of 8GB RAM

-Equipment: Webcam, speakers, and a microphone or headphones

-Internet Speed: Minimum of 100Mbps with a backup connection in case of outages



Work Details:

-Working Hours: Monday to Friday, 9 AM to 5:30 PM Pacific Standard Time | Tuesday to Saturday, 12 AM to 8:30 AM Manila Time

-Location: Anywhere in the Philippines



Why Join Us?

-Stable employment

-Competitive salary

-Bi-monthly salary

-A-Z benefits, including bonuses and annual raises

-Work-life balance 

-Work-from-home opportunity



Virtual Office Setup: We ask tea
mbers to maintain a virtual office via Google Meet with cameras on during work hours. This helps us simulate an in-office environment, encourages collaboration, and ensures everyone is present and engaged throughout the day, even while working remotely.



Note: The company uses a time tracker to ensure productivity and accountability.



Direct Hire:

This is a direct hire position — you will be employed and managed directly by our company, not through an agency or third party.



Important Note:

Do not send your application via our social media platforms or website contact forms. Failure to follow this instruction may result in disqualification.

title: Real Estate Operations Coordinator – Top 10 Advance!
description:
Real Estate Operations Coordinator – Top 10 Advance!



We’re a fast-growing land investing company, and we need someone to bring structure and 
ntum to our operations. You’ll work directly with the owner, be involved in real deals, and help us scale. You’ll also support our rental property business as needed.



If you’re organized, proactive, and excited to use AI to solve problems and move faster — we’ll give you the tools and opportunity to grow. This role has the potential to become an Operations Manager position as we scale.





What You’ll Be Doing



- Bookkeeping — categorize expenses, reconcile accounts, keep clean financials in QuickBooks

- Transaction coordination — work with title companies, vendors, and county offices to keep deals moving

- Follow-up & accountability — make sure tasks and deadlines don’t slip across the team

- KPI tracking — build simple reports so we always know where things stand

- Research — find realtors, vendors, and service providers as needed

- CRM management — keep deal data accurate and organized

- Automation — use AI tools to eliminate repetitive work and improve efficiency



You’re a Fit If Your



- Organized and detail-obsessed

- Proactive — you follow up without being asked

- Resourceful — you figure things out and get it done

- Comfortable making calls and working with professionals

- Ownership mindset — you don’t need daily hand-holding

- Growth-oriented — you want to learn and take on more over time

QuickBooks experience is a plus, but not required if you’re willing to learn.



The Details



- Full-time, US business hours, remote

- Reports to the owner, working alongside our sales lead

- Compensation: Competitive, based on experience



How to Apply

We don’t hire off resumes — we hire off results.



Complete our skills challenge. See your score and live ranking instantly on our public leaderboard. **Top 10 advance to a paid project task.**



[START THE CHALLENGE USING LINK BELOW]







We’re growing fast — show us you can grow with us.

title: Media Buyer (Facebook Ads) — E-commerce/Dropshipping
description:
Media Buyer 
 Ads) — E-commerce/Dropshipping



Remote • Part-time to Full-time Path



About Us

We’re a fast-growing e-commerce group operating multiple dropshipping brands. We combine rapid testing, sharp creative, and rigorous analytics to launch products that delight customers. You’ll join a lean, ambitious team where your work directly impacts revenue—and where high performers grow quickly.



The Role



As a Media Buyer, you’ll own the end-to-end execution of
 (Meta) acquisition campaigns across several storefronts. You’ll launch and scale daily tests, safeguard account health, and collaborate closely with our CEO, creative, and operations teams to hit revenue and efficiency targets.



What You’ll Do

- Plan, build, and launch campaigns in
 Ads Manager daily (audiences, budgets, placements, tracking).

- Manage and pace budgets against KPIs; reallocate spend toward winners in real time.

- QA all assets and flows pre-launch (ad copy/creatives, URLs, pixels, events, landing pages, checkout).

- Moderate ad comments (hide/delete/reply as appropriate) to protect brand and conversion.

- Monitor account health, policy compliance, and limits; escalate risks early.

- Maintain clear performance reporting in Google Sheets; summarize insights and next steps.

- Partner with the CEO, video editor, and EA; attend a weekly stand-up with concise updates.

- Proactively propose tests (creatives, angles, audiences, funnels) and process improvements.

- Stable account health and policy compliance.



What You Bring

- Minimum IQ of 120

- Fluent in English (written and spoken)

- 2+ years hands-on media buying for E-commerce on
/Meta (dropshipping experience is a plus).

- Strong performance marketing mindset: comfortable with hypotheses, rapid testing, and data-driven decisions.

- Advanced Ads Manager skills; working knowledge of Events Manager, pixels/API, ad account, audiences, and attribution.

- High attention to detail; excellent written English; organized and deadline-reliable.

- Ownership mentality—able to work independently, surface risks, and take initiative without micromanagement.

- Comfortable with Google Sheets (lookups, pivots, basic charting) and structured reporting.

- Note on assessments: We value analytical thinking. Short skills/aptitude tasks may be part of our selection process.



How We Work (and What You’ll Get)

- Part-time with flexible hours, results-oriented culture; path to full-time for top performers.

- Compensation: $5/hour to start + performance bonuses.

- Approved expense reimbursement.

- Merit-based raises as your impact grows.

- Access to proven SOP's, fast feedback loops, and direct collaboration with leadership.

- Real growth opportunities: broaden into creative strategy  or team management as we scale.



How to Apply

- Put your favorite animal in the subject line/title—this helps us spot detail-oriented applicants.

Please include:

- Your CV/Resume.

- Links or screenshots of recent e-commerce campaigns you’ve managed (redact sensitive data).

- A short paragraph on why you’re the best fit, including your typical testing framework (budget per test, iteration cadence, success/fail criteria).

- Your availability (hours per week) 

- Your IQ in your account (you can do this for free on onlinejobs)



No AI-generated applications—we read closely and can tell.



We’re excited to meet driven marketers who want to grow with us and take real ownership. If that sounds like you, let’s talk.

title: Credit Repair Specialist VA (Dispute Expert – DisputeFox Required)
description:
I am hiring an experienced Virtual Assistant for my credit repair business. This is a high-level role for someone who understands the credit repair process and can deliver strong, consistent results.



This is NOT an entry-level position. Only apply if you have real experience in credit repair and dispute processing.



Responsibilities include:

• Reviewing client credit reports  

• Identifying inaccurate, outdated, or unverifiable accounts  

• Sending and managing dispute rounds  

• Using DisputeFox to manage client files  

• Tracking results and updating client profiles  

• Escalating disputes when necessary (bureaus, CFPB, etc.)



Requirements:

• Proven experience in credit repair  

• Strong understanding of dispute strategies (factual, Metro 2, etc.)  

• Experience using DisputeFox (REQUIRED)  

• Knowledge of credit reporting laws (FCRA basics)  

• High attention to detail  

• Ability to work independently and meet deadlines  



I am looking for someone who is:

• Results-driven (focused on deletions, not just sending letters)  

• Reliable and consistent  

• Organized and professional  



To apply, include:

1. Your experience in credit repair  

2. Your experience with DisputeFox  

3. The dispute methods you use  

4. Examples of results you’ve achieved  



Only serious applicants with real experience will be considered.

title: Business Development Manager
description:
We're looking for a highly organized and relationship-focused Business Development Manager to help manage and grow a large network of professional contacts in the entertainment industry (music, sports, film/TV, digital). This role is about meaningful outreach, follow-up, and building long-term trust across a large portfolio.



If you're detail-oriented, a strong communicator, and excited by the world of entertainment, this role could be a great fit. Must have good English writing abilities without use of AI. 



What You’ll Be Doing:



- Manage a High Volume of Contacts: Oversee a large list of professional accounts, ensuring regular communication and accurate recordkeeping.

- Outreach & Relationship Building: Proactively reach out to contacts via
 and social media to stay connected, share updates, and strengthen professional relationships.

- Craft Well-Written Messages: Write polished, thoughtful
s and messages in English—clear, natural communication is key.

- Track Progress: Use tools like HubSpot to manage follow-ups, keep conversations organized, and hit relationship-building goals.

- Work Toward Clear Targets: While there’s no selling involved, this role does have measurable outreach and engagement goals.

- Think Creatively: Develop new approaches for keeping relationships active, warm, and productive over time.



Who We’re Looking For:

- Excellent written English—you’re comfortable writing professional messages and outreach
s with clarity and polish. - - -- Must have strong English writing and speaking abilities.

- Extremely organized—you can juggle many accounts and keep your outreach and follow-ups on track.

- Self-motivated and driven—you take initiative, meet goals, and stay focused without constant supervision.

- Relationship-minded—you enjoy connecting with people and maintaining positive, long-term communication.

- Familiarity with CRM tools like HubSpot is a plus.

- Spanish fluency is a plus, but not required.

- A genuine interest in the entertainment industry—especially music, sports, film/TV, or digital media—will help you succeed in this role.
